programs.programName: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)

verified.notes: Program officially renamed to H-HEAP (Hawaii Home Energy Assistance Program) as of current period. Income limits shown are monthly gross for 1 and 2 person households; no age requirement found in authoritative sources. Phone number is HCAP Central (Oahu primary office). Program serves elderly as vulnerable population but does not specify senior-specific eligibility criteria separate from general low-income households. No asset limits identified in search results. Application period for Energy Credit (EC) is June 2–30 annually; Energy Crisis Intervention (ECI) accepted year-round with limited monthly approvals.

programs.notes: Program officially renamed to H-HEAP (Hawaii Home Energy Assistance Program) as of current period. verified.notes: Income limit calculated as 200% of 2026 Federal Poverty Guidelines for Hawaii ($18,880 annual for household of 1 = $1573 monthly; $25,520 for 2 = $2127 monthly), per state policy as of April 2025[6]. No state-level application phone found; contact local subgrantees (e.g., HCAP 8085214531[2]). No asset test.

programs.programName: Retired Senior Volunteer Program (RSVP, related to SCSEP)

verified.notes: RSVP is a volunteer program with no income or asset tests. Phone number is for City & County of Honolulu RSVP program. Note: SCSEP (Senior Community Service Employment Program) is a separate paid training program with income limits (125% federal poverty level); RSVP is unpaid volunteer service. User query conflates these two distinct programs.
